title: pilea microphylla var. portulacoides
intro: Pilea microphylla var. portulacoides, also known as the Panama hat plant or creeping Charlie, is a low-growing, spreading plant with small, round leaves that is commonly used as a groundcover. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pilea microphylla var. portulacoides include its potential to reduce fever, treat skin conditions, and aid in digestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other plants to enhance its medicinal effects.
